figures.caption: Figure 1: Test AUROC on OASIS-3 MRI (higher is better) vs. number of trainable parameters (lower is better) for MIL and 3D CNN methods. Takeaway: Mean pooling MIL is almost as good and far more efficient than the best 3D CNN or MIL method. All MIL methods use the same pre-trained ViT encoder.
Table 2: Dataset statistics: Patient-scan counts by class.

Figure 3: Test AUROC for embedding-aggregation and prediction-aggregation on OASIS-3 CT (●), RSNA-1,149 CT (▲), ADNI1 MRI (■), and OASIS-3 MRI (◆). Takeaway: Results are concentrated around the y = x line, indicating the performance of embeddingaggregation and prediction-aggregation is comparable across pooling operations and datasets.

Figure 4: Test AUROC vs. train set size for semi-synthetic Shifted Mean task (Sec. 6), where aggregating subtle signals across 12 instances is needed. Takeaway: Even with 10,000 training scans, SmAP and TransMIL barely outperform Mean pooling and fall at least 0.04 AUROC below the best possible Bayes estimator.
Figure D.1: Slice-level attention analysis on test scans. The ground truth has attention uniformly distributed on positive slices and the positive slice images are outlined in red.
Table 4: Pooling comparison: Test AUROC on medium datasets. All MIL methods use a frozen ViT and embeddingaggregation. †3DINO was pre-trained on ADNI1, OASIS-3, and half of the RSNA ICH full dataset.
Table C.4: Test AUPRC on large datasets. All MIL methods use the embedding-aggregation approach.
Table C.3: Test AUPRC on moderately-sized datasets. All MIL methods use the prediction-aggregation approach.
Table C.2: Test AUPRC moderately-sized datasets. All MIL methods use the embedding-aggregation approach. †3DINO was pre-trained on ADNI1, OASIS-3, and half of the RSNA ICH full dataset.

Table 5: Pooling comparison: Test AUROC on Large datasets. All MIL methods use a frozen ViT and embeddingaggregation. Time is for training one NN at one hyperparameter on one train/test split. Results average over 3 data splits for RSNA-21,744 and 5 data splits for Proprietary-10k and show best of a grid search across many hyperparameters.
Table B.1: Test AUROC on moderately-sized datasets. All MIL methods use the prediction-aggregation approach.
